ogTitle: Boxwood Hill vs Boyanup: Climate Safety

heading: Boxwood Hill vs Boyanup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Boxwood Hill, Western Australia and Boyanup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boxwood Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Boyanup (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Boxwood Hill or Boyanup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Boxwood Hill has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Boyanup scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Boxwood Hill scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Boyanup scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Boxwood Hill or Boyanup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boxwood Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
